rulings.Case Title: JIM KELLY et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Dates For Depositions Of Defendant South City Lumber And Supply Companys Person(S) Most Qualified, Custodian(S) Of Records, And Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, August 8, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 2. Plaintiffs¿ motion to compel dates for depositions of defendant South City Lumber and Supply Company¿s person most qualified, custodian of records and production of documents is off calendar at the moving party¿s request. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JUAN CRUZ HINOJOSA et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION And Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories And Special Interrogatories From Plaintiffs
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, August 8, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Goulds Pumps, Inc.¿s motion to compel further responses to special interrogatories nos. 1 through 35 and to form interrogatory no. 17.1 is granted. Plaintiffs¿ responses are deficient. Further, as to form interrogatory no. 17.1, plaintiffs¿ response does not separately lay out each individual request to which it is responding. Plaintiffs have 10 days to provide further responses. Defendant¿s request for sanctions of $1620 is granted. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30am, not 9:00am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
